content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 3 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 198–200
**LXX Reference:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 145–146
**Disagreement Log:** `disagreements/DEU_003.md`

---

## Council Notes

Chapter 3 narrates the conquest of Og of Bashan, the distribution of the Transjordanian lands to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh, the instructions for the remaining tribes to assist in the western conquest, and Moses' personal prayer to see the land — followed by the Lord's firm refusal. The chapter closes with the encampment at Beth-peor.

All three translators agreed on the core narrative and the central theological moment of Moses' prayer and denial (vv.23–28). Key rulings: "Rephaim" (not translated) for the giant peoples; "Havvoth-jair" preserved as a proper name; "devoted to destruction" for *cherem*; "O Lord GOD" (*Adonai YHWH*) as the double divine address; the four compass directions named in full; "Enough!" for *rav lakh*.

The LXX Vaticanus at v.25 reads *Ἀντιλίβανον* (Anti-Lebanon) rather than *Λίβανον* (Lebanon) — a variant not in the MT. SOPHIA follows the MT: "Lebanon."

---

## Consensus Translation

1 "Then we turned and went up by the way of Bashan, and Og king of Bashan came out against us — he and all his people — to battle at Edrei.

2 And the LORD said to me: 'Do not fear him, for I have given him and all his people and his land into your hand, and you shall do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorites who dwelt in Heshbon.'

3 So the LORD our God gave into our hand Og also, king of Bashan, and all his people, and we struck him down until not a survivor was left to him.

4 And we captured all his cities at that time — there was not a city that we did not take from them: sixty cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

5 All these were fortified cities with high walls, gates, and bars — besides very many unwalled towns.

6 And we devoted them to destruction, as we did to Sihon king of Heshbon — every city devoted to destruction: men, women, and children.

7 But all the livestock and the spoil of the cities we plundered for ourselves.

8 So we took at that time from the hand of the two Amorite kings the land that is beyond the Jordan, from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon —

9 (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion, and the Amorites call it Senir) —

10 all the cities of the tableland, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, as far as Salecah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

11 For only Og king of Bashan remained of the remnant of the Rephaim. Behold, his bed was a bed of iron — is it not in Rabbah of the sons of Ammon? Nine cubits was its length and four cubits its width, by the cubit of a man.

12 This land we took possession of at that time: from Aroer, which is by the Wadi Arnon, and half the hill country of Gilead and its cities, I gave to the Reubenites and the Gadites.

13 And the rest of Gilead and all Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I gave to half the tribe of Manasseh — all the region of Argob. All that Bashan is called the land of the Rephaim.

14 Jair son of Manasseh took all the region of Argob as far as the boundary of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and called them — the Bashan — after his own name, Havvoth-jair, to this day.

15 To Machir I gave Gilead.

16 And to the Reubenites and the Gadites I gave from Gilead as far as the Wadi Arnon — the middle of the wadi as the boundary — and as far as the Wadi Jabbok, which is the boundary of the sons of Ammon;

17 the Arabah also, and the Jordan as the boundary, from Chinnereth as far as the sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, under the slopes of Pisgah on the east.

18 And I commanded you at that time, saying: 'The LORD your God has given you this land to possess. You shall cross over armed before your brothers the sons of Israel, all the men of valor.

19 Only your wives and your little ones and your livestock — I know that you have much livestock — shall dwell in your cities that I have given you,

20 until the LORD gives rest to your brothers as to you, and they also take possession of the land that the LORD your God is giving them beyond the Jordan. Then each of you may return to his possession that I have given you.'

21 And I commanded Joshua at that time, saying: 'Your eyes have seen all that the LORD your God has done to these two kings; so shall the LORD do to all the kingdoms into which you are crossing over.

22 You shall not fear them, for it is the LORD your God who fights for you.'

23 "And I pleaded with the LORD at that time, saying:

24 'O Lord GOD, you have begun to show your servant your greatness and your strong hand — for what god is there in the heavens or on the earth who can do deeds and mighty acts as you do?

25 Let me cross over, please, and see the good land that is beyond the Jordan — this good hill country and Lebanon.'

26 But the LORD was angry with me on your account and would not listen to me, and the LORD said to me: 'Enough! Do not speak to me again about this matter.

27 Go up to the top of Pisgah and lift up your eyes westward and northward and southward and eastward, and look with your eyes, for you shall not cross this Jordan.

28 But charge Joshua, and strengthen him and encourage him, for he shall cross over before this people, and he shall cause them to inherit the land that you shall see.'

29 So we remained in the valley opposite Beth-peor.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Ruling Notes

**V.1 — "went up by the way" vs. "went up the road to":** EMET: "went up the way of the Bashan." LOGOS: "went up by the way of Bashan." PNEUMA: "went up the road to Bashan." SOPHIA: "went up by the way of Bashan" (LOGOS) — the Hebrew *derekh ha-Bashan* is "the way of Bashan," and the preposition "by" is natural English.

**V.2 — "I have given" vs. "I have handed him over":** EMET: "into your hand I have given him." LOGOS: "I have given him...into your hand." PNEUMA: "I have handed him over to you." SOPHIA: "I have given him...into your hand" (LOGOS) — *natatti beyad'kha* is the standard formula; "handed over" (PNEUMA) is acceptable but less precise.

**V.4 — "sixty cities, all the region" — parenthetical structure:** EMET/LOGOS: "sixty cities, all the region of Argob." PNEUMA: "sixty cities, the entire region of Argob." "All" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "entire" (PNEUMA) — both are faithful; SOPHIA adopts "all" as closer to *kol*.

**V.6 — "devoted them to destruction" / "every city devoted to destruction":** EMET: "devoted to destruction as we did to Sichon...devoting to destruction every city." LOGOS: "devoted them to destruction...every city devoted to destruction." PNEUMA: "completely destroyed them...every city, men, women, and children." SOPHIA: "devoted them to destruction...every city devoted to destruction" (LOGOS) — preserving the double *cherem* construction as in the MT.

**V.9 — "Sidonians" vs. "Tsidoni'im":** EMET: "Tsidoni'im." LOGOS/PNEUMA: "Sidonians." SOPHIA: "Sidonians" — conventional English form.

**V.11 — "bed of iron" vs. "iron bed" / "sons of Ammon" vs. "children of Ammon":** EMET/LOGOS: "his bed was a bed of iron." PNEUMA: "His bed was made of iron." The Hebrew *arso arset barzel* has the emphatic repetition. SOPHIA: "his bed was a bed of iron" — the Hebrew double noun (*eres...areset*) is preserved by EMET/LOGOS's repetition. "Sons of Ammon" per project convention.

**V.13 — "which is called all of Bashan" / "All that Bashan is called":** EMET: "all that Bashan is called 'the land of the Refa'im.'" LOGOS: "That is called the land of the Rephaim." PNEUMA treats this parenthetically. SOPHIA: "All that Bashan is called the land of the Rephaim" — clarifying the referent of "that."

**V.14 — "Havvoth-jair":** All three translators preserve this as a proper name, though with slight variation. SOPHIA: "Havvoth-jair" per LOGOS — the standard English transliteration.

**V.17 — "Chinnereth" vs. "Kinneret":** EMET: "Kinneret." LOGOS: "Chinnereth." PNEUMA: "Kinnereth." SOPHIA: "Chinnereth" (LOGOS) — the conventional English transliteration of this place name.

**V.18 — "men of valor" vs. "able men" / "all the able men":** EMET: "all the able men." LOGOS: "all the men of valor." PNEUMA: "All your fighting men." The Hebrew *kol bney chayil* — "all the sons of valor." SOPHIA: "all the men of valor" (LOGOS) — more precise than "able men" or "fighting men."

**V.22 — "it is the LORD your God who fights for you" vs. "the LORD your God — he is fighting for you":** EMET: "the LORD your God — he is fighting for you." LOGOS: "it is the LORD your God who fights for you." PNEUMA: "the LORD your God himself will fight for you." SOPHIA: "it is the LORD your God who fights for you" (LOGOS) — the emphasis particle *hu* is reflected in "it is."

**V.23 — "pleaded" vs. "entreated":** EMET: "I entreated the LORD." LOGOS/PNEUMA: "I pleaded with the LORD." The Hebrew *va-etchannan* (Hithpa'el of *chanan*) — to implore grace, plead for favor. SOPHIA: "I pleaded with the LORD" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) — "pleaded" is more natural English; "entreated" is also acceptable.

**V.24 — "O Lord GOD" / "O Sovereign LORD":** EMET/LOGOS: "O Lord GOD." PNEUMA: "O Sovereign LORD." The Hebrew *Adonai YHWH* — the double divine address. SOPHIA: "O Lord GOD" (EMET/LOGOS) — conventional formal rendering that distinguishes the two titles clearly. PNEUMA's "Sovereign" is interpretive.

**V.24 — "strong hand" vs. "mighty hand" vs. "powerful hand":** EMET: "your strong hand." LOGOS: "your mighty hand." PNEUMA: "your powerful hand." Hebrew *chazaqah yad'kha* = strong hand. SOPHIA: "your strong hand" (EMET) — *chazaq* = strong, the standard Deuteronomic term.

**V.25 — "this good hill country and Lebanon" vs. "that beautiful hill country and Lebanon":** EMET/LOGOS: "this good hill country and Lebanon." PNEUMA: "that beautiful hill country and Lebanon." Hebrew *ha-har ha-tov ha-zeh* — "this good mountain." SOPHIA: "this good hill country and Lebanon" (EMET/LOGOS). The demonstrative *zeh* (this) is proximate; "beautiful" (PNEUMA) is interpretive.

**V.26 — "Enough!" vs. "Enough for you" vs. "Enough of that!":** EMET: "Enough for you — do not add to speak to me further of this matter." LOGOS: "Enough of that! Do not speak to me again about this matter." PNEUMA: "Enough! Do not speak to me again about this matter." SOPHIA: "Enough! Do not speak to me again about this matter" (PNEUMA/LOGOS combination) — abrupt divine closure is best rendered with the single exclamation.

**V.27 — "lift up your eyes...westward and northward and southward and eastward":** All three translate the four directions. EMET: "westward and northward and southward and eastward." LOGOS/PNEUMA similar. SOPHIA follows this order faithfully — the Hebrew gives the four compass points in this sequence (*yammah ve-tsaphonah ve-temanah u-mizrachah*).

**V.28 — "charge Joshua" vs. "command Joshua":** EMET: "command Yehoshua." LOGOS: "charge Joshua." PNEUMA: "commission Joshua." The Hebrew *tsav* = command, charge. SOPHIA: "charge Joshua" (LOGOS) — fits the solemn, commissioning context.

**V.28 — "strengthen him and encourage him":** All three preserve these two imperatives. SOPHIA: "strengthen him and encourage him" — standard rendering consistent with LOGOS.

---

## Textual Notes for the Consensus Record

**V.11 — Og's bed:** The parenthetical about Og's iron bed/sarcophagus in Rabbah serves as physical evidence of the giant's historical reality. The LXX Vaticanus renders *klinē sidēra* — "iron bed/couch." EMET notes the possibility that *eres* may refer to a sarcophagus (Akkadian cognate). SOPHIA preserves "bed of iron" without editorializing on this question.

**V.25 — LXX variant at Lebanon:** Vaticanus reads *Ἀντιλίβανον* (Anti-Lebanon) rather than *Λίβανον* (Lebanon). This is a notable LXX expansion not in the MT. SOPHIA follows the MT.

**Vv.23–27 — Moses denied entry:** Deuteronomy presents this exclusion as caused "on your account" (*biglalkem*) — linking Moses' fate to the people's sin. This is the most personal passage in the book to this point. The actual cause (Numbers 20) is not rehearsed; Moses attributes his fate to solidarity with the sinning generation. The ambiguity is preserved.
